message:
mcp-server: --demo-backend flag — public demo answers real queries

After the worker-pool fix (commit 11f82ae), the live Cloud Run demo
still refused every rulake_query / list_collections / publish_bundle
because no backends were registered. The witness chain correctly
refuses to serve from an empty backend set (POLICY_REFUSED_ALLOWLIST),
which is the right behaviour for production but useless for a public
demo where the whole point is "type one query, see something work".

Fix: add --demo-backend CLI flag (and demo_backend = true config field).
When set, RuLakeMcpServer::new injects:

  - a deterministic LocalBackend("demo") with one seeded collection
    "memory" — 100 vectors at D=8, PCG32 seed 0xDEADBEEF (witness
    reproducible across restarts AND across operators)
  - an AllowBlock { backend: "demo", collection: ".*",
                    caps: ["read", "publish"] }

Result: a fresh `rulake_query intent=search target.routes=[["demo","memory"]]`
returns real witness-anchored hits. /rulake-memory:memory-recall
against the demo wire becomes useful instead of aspirational.

The flag is off by default — production deploys never get the toy
collection. Documented in docs/deploy/cloud-run.md §Step 3 with a
warning that the witness over a 100-row toy isn't useful in prod.

Tests:
  - demo_backend_registers_seeded_collection_and_allow_block ✓
  - demo_backend_off_by_default ✓
Plus the existing 40 unit tests still green.

Operator action to make the live demo actually serve queries:

  # Rebuild the image (picks up worker-pool fix + --demo-backend)
  gcloud builds submit . --project=ruv-dev --region=us-central1 \
    --config=/tmp/cloudbuild-mcp.yaml

  # Redeploy with the new flag in args
  gcloud run services update rulake-mcp-demo \
    --region=us-central1 --project=ruv-dev \
    --image=us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/ruv-dev/cloud-run-source-deploy/rulake-mcp-demo:latest \
    --command=/usr/local/bin/rulake-mcp \
    --args="^|^http|--bind|0.0.0.0:8080|--auth|none|--insecure-allow-no-auth|--capabilities|read,publish,admin|--demo-backend"

After redeploy, all 8 rulake tools become live on https://rulake-mcp.ruv.io/.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -46,6 +46,20 @@ pub struct McpConfig {
     /// must match at least one block.
     #[serde(default, rename = "allow")]
     pub allow: Vec<AllowBlock>,
+
+    /// When true, register a deterministic in-memory `LocalBackend`
+    /// named `"demo"` with one seeded collection (`memory`, 100
+    /// vectors at D=8, PCG32 seed = 0xDEADBEEF) and add an
+    /// allow-block granting `read,publish` against
+    /// `(backend="demo", collection=".*")`. Lets the public Cloud
+    /// Run demo answer real `rulake_query` / `rulake_list_collections`
+    /// / `rulake_publish_bundle` calls instead of refusing on an
+    /// empty allowlist.
+    ///
+    /// Off in production. Enable via `--demo-backend` on the CLI or
+    /// `demo_backend = true` in mcp.toml.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub demo_backend: bool,
 }
 
 /// One [[allow]] block in mcp.toml.

@@ -89,10 +89,23 @@ gcloud run deploy rulake-mcp-demo \
   --timeout=60s \
   --set-env-vars="RUST_LOG=info" \
   --command=/usr/local/bin/rulake-mcp \
-  --args="^|^http|--bind|0.0.0.0:8080|--auth|none|--insecure-allow-no-auth|--capabilities|read,publish,admin"
+  --args="^|^http|--bind|0.0.0.0:8080|--auth|none|--insecure-allow-no-auth|--capabilities|read,publish,admin|--demo-backend"
 ```
 
-Three things in there are non-obvious:
+Four things in there are non-obvious:
+
+> **Gotcha — `--demo-backend`.** Without this flag the demo deploy ships
+> with **zero registered backends**. Every `rulake_query` then refuses
+> with `POLICY_REFUSED_ALLOWLIST` (the witness chain refuses to serve
+> from an empty backend set), and `rulake_list_collections` returns
+> empty. With `--demo-backend`, the server registers a deterministic
+> `LocalBackend("demo")` with one seeded collection (`memory`, 100
+> vectors at D=8, PCG32 seed `0xDEADBEEF`) and adds a matching
+> `[[allow]]` block granting `read,publish` on `(demo, .*)`. Result:
+> a fresh `rulake_query intent=search target.routes=[["demo","memory"]]`
+> returns real witness-anchored hits out of the box, with no further
+> operator action. Off in production deploys (the witness over a 100-row
+> toy collection isn't useful there).
 
 > **Gotcha — `--port=8080` + hardcoded bind.** Cloud Run sets `PORT`
 > dynamically; the container is supposed to listen on `$PORT`. Our
